skill.name: shadcn
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through shadcn/ui projects, including project inspection, component selection, CLI usage, registry workflows, styling rules, and component composition.
skill.summary: Build, update, debug, and style shadcn/ui components with project-aware guidance.
skill.author: shadcn

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many hits, but most are false positives from Markdown code fences, prose, URLs, and UI examples. The confirmed risk is legitimate shadcn behavior: package-manager commands, registry access, and project file changes, which warrant a medium-risk warning but do not show malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Package Manager Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally runs and recommends package-manager commands such as npx, pnpm dlx, and bunx. This is expected for shadcn workflows, but it can fetch packages and modify project files, so users should review commands before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are explicit and central to the skill workflow. They appear legitimate for shadcn management, but they still execute external tooling and can write files.

medium_findings.title: Remote Registry and Documentation Access
medium_findings.description: The skill documents use of remote URLs for registry items, component docs, examples, and custom registries. This is legitimate, but remote content can affect installed source files and should be inspected before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in documentation and match the advertised registry workflow. No malicious endpoint is evident, but remote registry use is a real supply-chain risk.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography hits are in metadata, Markdown prose, or UI examples. I found no cryptographic operation, hashing routine, or security-sensitive crypto choice at the cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptions or CLI option tables, not cryptographic code. The scanner appears to have matched short substrings inside ordinary text.

low_findings.title: Path and Hidden File Detections Are Documentation Context
low_findings.description: The path traversal and hidden-file hits are documentation references, such as relative Markdown links and an editor configuration path. They are not runtime path construction or arbitrary file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are static documentation examples. They do not read, write, normalize, or execute paths from user-controlled input.

low_findings.title: Network Scanner Tool Detections Are Identifier Matches
low_findings.description: The network scanning detections occur in icon examples using iconMap and StatusBadge identifiers. I found no network scanning command, target enumeration, or reconnaissance workflow there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are React icon component examples. The suspicious pattern is a substring match inside a variable name, not a tool invocation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Manager Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill intentionally runs and recommends package-manager commands such as npx, pnpm dlx, and bunx. This is expected for shadcn workflows, but it can fetch packages and modify project files, so users should review commands before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are explicit and central to the skill workflow. They appear legitimate for shadcn management, but they still execute external tooling and can write files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Registry and Documentation Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents use of remote URLs for registry items, component docs, examples, and custom registries. This is legitimate, but remote content can affect installed source files and should be inspected before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in documentation and match the advertised registry workflow. No malicious endpoint is evident, but remote registry use is a real supply-chain risk.

content.value_statement: shadcn/ui projects need precise component APIs, aliases, and styling rules. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use the CLI and project context correctly.
actual_capabilities: Reads shadcn project context with the CLI info command.
Selects installed and registry components before creating custom UI.
Guides safe use of init, add, search, view, docs, and diff commands.
Applies shadcn composition rules for forms, dialogs, cards, tabs, icons, and overlays.
Adapts imports and behavior to radix or base primitives, icon library, aliases, and Tailwind version.
Reviews third-party registry components for imports, missing subcomponents, and style violations.
limitations: Requires a project with Node tooling and access to the shadcn CLI.
May need network access for registry searches, documentation, and component installation.
Does not replace human review before overwriting existing customized components.
Depends on current shadcn CLI behavior and registry availability.

use_cases.title: Create Product UI
use_cases.description: Build forms, dialogs, dashboards, and navigation with correct shadcn/ui composition and styling.

use_cases.title: Upgrade Components
use_cases.description: Preview upstream changes with dry runs and diffs before merging component updates into a project.

use_cases.title: Adopt Registries
use_cases.description: Search built-in and community registries, inspect component files, and adjust imports for local aliases.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a shadcn/ui card and button to this page. Use my project aliases and follow the installed component style.

prompt_templates.title: Build Form UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a settings form with validation states. Use FieldGroup, Field, semantic colors, and the configured icon library.

prompt_templates.title: Preview Registry Install
prompt_templates.prompt: Find a login block in the registry I name, preview the files it would add, and explain the changes before installing.

prompt_templates.title: Merge Component Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Update my customized dialog component from upstream. Use dry-run and diff first, then preserve my local changes.

output_examples.input: Create a dashboard with stat cards and loading states.
output_examples.output: The assistant returns a dashboard plan and component implementation using Card composition, Badge status labels, Skeleton loading states, and gap-based layout.

output_examples.input: Switch my project to a preset code.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks whether to reinstall, merge, or skip existing components, then runs the preset workflow inside the project directory.

output_examples.input: Review a third-party registry component before installing it.
output_examples.output: The assistant previews the registry item, checks imports and dependencies, and flags any mismatch with aliases, icon library, or component rules.

best_practices: Run project context commands before choosing imports, paths, or primitive APIs.
Use dry-run and diff before overwriting or updating existing components.
Inspect third-party registry files before trusting their imports, icons, or composition.
anti_patterns: Do not fetch raw component source manually when the shadcn CLI can resolve it.
Do not assume lucide icons, radix primitives, or default aliases without checking project context.
Do not overwrite customized components without explicit user approval.

faq.question: Does this skill install components automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide CLI install commands, but destructive or overwrite actions should require clear user approval.

faq.question: Does it support community registries?
faq.answer: Yes. It can search, preview, and install registry items when the registry is specified or configured.

faq.question: Can it work without network access?
faq.answer: Partially. Local project checks may work, but registry searches, docs, and installs usually need network access.

faq.question: Does it handle Tailwind v3 and v4?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks the project context and uses the correct CSS or config workflow.

faq.question: Why is this skill medium risk?
faq.answer: It intentionally runs package-manager commands and may fetch registry content or write project files.

faq.question: Can it create custom components?
faq.answer: Yes, but it prefers existing shadcn/ui components and registry items before custom markup.
